title: pleurosorus rutifolius subsp. subglandulosus
intro: Pleurosorus rutifolius subsp. subglandulosus, also known as a subspecies of the Lichenous fern Pleurosorus rutifolius, is a rare and unique plant species that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Pleurosorus rutifolius subsp. subglandulosus include its ability to boost the immune system, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Pleurosorus rutifolius subsp. subglandulosus often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by drying and powdering the plant material, then mixing it with a solvent such as water or ethanol to create a medicinal extract.
